page: Vultr vx1-g-96c-384g is a virtual machine instance within the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD processor with 96 vCPUs and 96 physical cores, operating at one thread per core. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance relies on network-attached storage, with a minimal local storage allocation of 1 GB. It includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. A key architectural tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation model, meaning physical CPU resources are shared with other tenants on the host. This shared allocation can introduce resource contention, making the instance less suitable for workloads requiring sustained, predictable maximum CPU performance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU model allows for a high density of vCPUs and memory at a lower hardware class tier compared to dedicated-core instances. This resource profile is designed for general-purpose workloads that require large memory capacity and high core counts but experience bursty or intermittent CPU utilization. Appropriate workloads include web serving, development environments, distributed testing, and general-purpose applications that do not require dedicated CPU guarantees.
description: Vultr vx1-g-96c-384g is an x86_64 virtual server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, configured with 96 AMD vCPUs operating at one thread per core and 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance utilizes a shared CPU allocation model, which offers high resource density but introduces potential performance variability due to shared host resources. Storage is network-based, with only 1 GB of local storage allocated, and the instance includes one public IPv4 address. Lacking dedicated GPUs or local NVMe arrays, this configuration is designed for general-purpose workloads that benefit from high core counts and substantial memory capacity without requiring continuous, dedicated CPU performance. It is suitable for web serving, staging environments, distributed testing, and general-purpose applications where resource demands are intermittent.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-96c-384g is a VX1 Cloud Compute instance featuring 96 shared AMD vCPUs, 384.0 GB of RAM, and network-attached storage, designed for cost-effective general-purpose workloads.

tagline: A high-core, shared-CPU virtual server offering balanced memory and compute resources for non-continuous, bursty general-purpose workloads.

384.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core.
